tasks.label: BlueZ: start private debug bus
tasks.command: bus_dir="${workspaceFolder}/build/debug/run/dbus" && bus_pid="$bus_dir/bus.pid" && bus_info="$bus_dir/bus.info" && bus_socket="$bus_dir/system_bus_socket" && mkdir -p "$bus_dir" && if [ -f "$bus_pid" ] && kill -0 "$(cat "$bus_pid")" 2>/dev/null; then kill "$(cat "$bus_pid")"; fi && rm -f "$bus_socket" "$bus_pid" "$bus_info" && addr="unix:path=$bus_socket" && info="$(dbus-daemon --session --fork --address="$addr" --print-address=1 --print-pid=1)" && printf '%s\n' "$info" > "$bus_info" && printf '%s\n' "$info" | sed -n '2p' > "$bus_pid"

tasks.label: BlueZ: inspect vhci host state (sudo)
tasks.command:
sudo sh -c 'printf "modules:\
"; grep -E "^(hci_vhci|vhci_hcd) " /proc/modules || true; printf "\nproc-misc:\
"; grep -n vhci /proc/misc || true; printf "\nsysfs-misc:\
"; ls -l /sys/class/misc | grep vhci || true; printf "\ndevnode:\
"; ls -l /dev/vhci || true; printf "\nudev rule:\
"; sed -n "1,20p" /etc/udev/rules.d/99-bluez-vhci.rules 2>/dev/null || echo missing'

tasks.label: BlueZ: install vhci udev rule (sudo)
tasks.command: sudo install -m 0644 "${workspaceFolder}/.vscode/99-bluez-vhci.rules" /etc/udev/rules.d/99-bluez-vhci.rules && sudo udevadm control --reload && sudo udevadm trigger --subsystem-match=misc --sysname-match=vhci && sudo udevadm settle && ls -l /dev/vhci

tasks.label: BlueZ: check profile-tester prerequisites
tasks.command: echo 'Inspecting emulator prerequisites'; printf 'user: '; id -un; printf 'groups: '; id -nG; printf 'hci_vhci: '; if grep -q '^hci_vhci ' /proc/modules; then echo loaded; else echo not-loaded; fi; printf 'vhci_hcd: '; if grep -q '^vhci_hcd ' /proc/modules; then echo loaded; else echo not-loaded; fi; if [ -e /dev/vhci ]; then echo '/dev/vhci: present'; ls -l /dev/vhci; test -r /dev/vhci && echo 'read access: yes' || echo 'read access: no'; test -w /dev/vhci && echo 'write access: yes' || echo 'write access: no'; else echo '/dev/vhci: missing'; echo 'Missing /dev/vhci. Load the Bluetooth hci_vhci kernel module or run emulator-backed tests in a VM/test-runner environment.' >&2; exit 1; fi
